assessment_items.question_content: **Frank Lloyd Wright emphasized the house's connection with nature by the liberal use of [[☃ dropdown 1]].**
hints:
**Frank Lloyd Wright emphasized the house's connection with nature by the liberal use of glass. The house has no walls facing the falls, only a central stone core for fireplaces and stone columns.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/63876e963352c4993b9cf00de73123ac.jpg)

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/architecture/a/frank-lloyd-wright-fallingwater)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**Fallingwater is the crowning achievement of Frank Lloyd Wright's domestic architecture. If you look at all of his earlier buildings, they lead toward the organic architecture and styles used in Fallingwater.**

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/63876e963352c4993b9cf00de73123ac.jpg)

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
**Wright was influenced by many architectural styles, but Fallingwater owes little to his previous designs. In this house, Wright responds to European Modernist design.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/architecture/a/frank-lloyd-wright-fallingwater)**

assessment_items.question_content:
**In Fallingwater, Frank Lloyd Wright created long suspended balconies made of [[☃ dropdown 1]], which were revolutionary for their time.**


![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/63876e963352c4993b9cf00de73123ac.jpg)
hints:
**The suspended balconies were made with reinforced concrete.**

**[Read this essay to review](/humanities/art-history/art-history-1907-1960-age-of-global-conflict/architecture/a/frank-lloyd-wright-fallingwater)**
